message:
fix(fr-command): redis.error_reply prepends ERR code when missing [bd-frankenredis-xvlj]

Lua's redis.error_reply("err") was returning a wire reply of just
"err" (no error code) because the binding stored the raw argument
verbatim in the {err=...} table.

Mirror upstream script_lua.c::luaRedisErrorReplyCommand +
luaPushErrorBuff:
  - prepend '-' if the input doesn't start with one
  - parse '-CODE msg' (split by first space): if no space after
    '-CODE', fall back to default code "ERR" with the entire body
    as the message
  - the {err=...} table value is "<CODE> <msg>", which
    luaReplyToRedisReply emits as "-<CODE> <msg>\r\n" on the wire
  - trim trailing CR/LF

Verified byte-for-byte against vendored redis 7.2.4:
  EVAL "return redis.error_reply('err')" 0 → "ERR err"
  EVAL "return redis.error_reply('-CUSTOM hi')" 0 → "CUSTOM hi"
  EVAL "return redis.error_reply('NOMSG')" 0 → "ERR NOMSG"
  EVAL "return redis.error_reply('-NOMSG')" 0 → "ERR NOMSG"
  EVAL "return redis.error_reply('NOAUTH custom message')" 0
    → "ERR NOAUTH custom message"

Pinned by eval_redis_error_reply_prepends_err_code_when_missing in
fr-command. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
